mcqs.question: What is the primary function of a C++ compiler?

options.text: To execute C++ code
To translate C++ code into machine code
To debug C++ code
To optimize C++ code

mcqs.explanation: A C++ compiler translates C++ source code into machine code.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the `g++` command in C++?

options.text: To compile and link C++ source code
To execute C++ code
To debug C++ code
To optimize C++ code

mcqs.explanation: The `g++` command is used to compile and link C++ source code.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between a compiler and an interpreter?

options.text: A compiler translates source code into machine code before execution, while an interpreter translates source code into machine code during execution
A compiler translates source code into machine code during execution, while an interpreter translates source code into machine code before execution
A compiler is faster than an interpreter
An interpreter is faster than a compiler

mcqs.explanation: A compiler translates source code into machine code before execution, while an interpreter translates source code into machine code during execution.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the `-o` flag in the `g++` command?

options.text: To specify the output file name for the compiled executable
To compile the source code without linking
To include debugging information in the compiled executable
To optimize the compiled executable

mcqs.explanation: The `-o` flag specifies the output file name for the compiled executable.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the `-c` flag in the `g++` command?

options.text: To compile the source code without linking
To specify the output file name for the compiled executable
To include debugging information in the compiled executable
To optimize the compiled executable

mcqs.explanation: The `-c` flag compiles the source code without linking.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the `-g` flag in the `g++` command?

options.text: To include debugging information in the compiled executable
To specify the output file name for the compiled executable
To compile the source code without linking
To optimize the compiled executable

mcqs.explanation: The `-g` flag includes debugging information in the compiled executable.

mcqs.question: What is the advantage of compiling C++ code?

options.text: Faster execution and error checking before runtime
Slower execution and error checking during runtime
Faster compilation and error checking during runtime
Slower compilation and error checking before runtime

mcqs.explanation: Compiling C++ code provides faster execution and error checking before runtime.

mcqs.question: What is the disadvantage of compiling C++ code?

options.text: Extra step before execution and can be time-consuming for large programs
Faster execution and error checking before runtime
Slower execution and error checking during runtime
Faster compilation and error checking during runtime

mcqs.explanation: Compiling C++ code requires an extra step before execution and can be time-consuming for large programs.

mcqs.question: What is the role of a linker in C++?

options.text: To resolve external references and libraries in the compiled code
To compile the source code without linking
To specify the output file name for the compiled executable
To include debugging information in the compiled executable

mcqs.explanation: The linker resolves external references and libraries in the compiled code.

mcqs.question: What is the role of a loader in C++?

options.text: To load the executable file into memory for execution
To compile the source code without linking
To specify the output file name for the compiled executable
To include debugging information in the compiled executable

mcqs.explanation: The loader loads the executable file into memory for execution.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between a compiler and a loader?

options.text: A compiler translates source code into machine code, while a loader loads the executable file into memory for execution
A compiler loads the executable file into memory for execution, while a loader translates source code into machine code
A compiler is faster than a loader
A loader is faster than a compiler

mcqs.explanation: A compiler translates source code into machine code, while a loader loads the executable file into memory for execution.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the `g++` command with the `-c` flag?

options.text: To compile the source code without linking
To specify the output file name for the compiled executable
To include debugging information in the compiled executable
To optimize the compiled executable

mcqs.explanation: The `g++` command with the `-c` flag compiles the source code without linking.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the `g++` command with the `-g` flag?

options.text: To include debugging information in the compiled executable
To specify the output file name for the compiled executable
To compile the source code without linking
To optimize the compiled executable

mcqs.explanation: The `g++` command with the `-g` flag includes debugging information in the compiled executable.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the `g++` command with the `-o` flag?

options.text: To specify the output file name for the compiled executable
To compile the source code without linking
To include debugging information in the compiled executable
To optimize the compiled executable

mcqs.explanation: The `g++` command with the `-o` flag specifies the output file name for the compiled executable.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the `g++` command with the `-c` and `-g` flags?

options.text: To compile the source code without linking and include debugging information in the compiled executable
To specify the output file name for the compiled executable and optimize the compiled executable
To compile the source code without linking and optimize the compiled executable
To include debugging information in the compiled executable and specify the output file name for the compiled executable

mcqs.explanation: The `g++` command with the `-c` and `-g` flags compiles the source code without linking and includes debugging information in the compiled executable.
